headline: The NARRATE stage: read a repo's generated wiki (deepwiki-open, local/Ollama) as a content-addressed receipt -- the narrator's prose explanation of what a repo IS. Fast cached-read only (mirrors code-recon orient); the join key is sha256 over the scrubbed wiki, so a NARRATE claim can be pinned to a code-recon PROVER verification.

capabilities.label: Read a repo's cached deepwiki wiki, content-addressed (read-only)
capabilities.evidence: C:/Users/bruke/Pre Atlas/tools/deepwiki/ask.py wraps deepwiki-open GET /api/wiki_cache (C:/Users/bruke/deepwiki-open/api/api.py:463); content-address = sha256 of the wiki JSON with volatile keys scrubbed
capabilities.notes: Read-only. {repo} is a github/gitlab URL or a local path (forward slashes; charset-safe -- no '*'). found:false (exit 0) when the backend is down OR no wiki is cached for the repo (a legitimate absence, like orient MISSING) -- generate the wiki out-of-band first (deepwiki-open is Ollama-slow, never every-commit). Live RAG Q&A (--ask) is STANDALONE-only and never reached through the gateway: it runs Ollama for tens of seconds (blows the 20s timeout) and a stochastic answer has no stable content-address. A wiki is a NARRATOR artifact -- confabulates; prove with code-recon before acting.
